stem: In a right triangle with hypotenuse 20 and one acute angle of 30 degrees, an altitude is drawn from the right-angle vertex to the hypotenuse. What is the area of the smaller of the two triangles created by this altitude?
explanation: In the 30-60-90 right triangle with hypotenuse 20: the shorter leg (opposite 30 degrees) is 10 and the longer leg (opposite 60 degrees) is 10sqrt(3). The altitude h from the right-angle vertex C to hypotenuse AB satisfies: Area of triangle = (1/2)(10)(10sqrt(3)) = 50sqrt(3), and also Area = (1/2)(AB)(h) = (1/2)(20)(h) = 10h. So h = 5sqrt(3). The altitude foot D divides the hypotenuse into segments: DB = (shorter leg)^2 / hypotenuse = 100/20 = 5, and DA = (longer leg)^2 / hypotenuse = 300/20 = 15. Check: 5 + 15 = 20. The smaller sub-triangle has base DB = 5 and height h = 5sqrt(3). Its area is (1/2)(5)(5sqrt(3)) = 25sqrt(3)/2.

distractorRationale.A: Correctly finds the altitude foot divides the hypotenuse with the shorter segment DB = 5, but confuses the altitude h = 5sqrt(3) with the shorter leg of the original triangle (10), computing area as (1/2)(5)(10) = 25
distractorRationale.B: Correct: Finds the legs as 10 and 10sqrt(3), computes the altitude to the hypotenuse as h = 5sqrt(3), identifies the shorter segment DB = 5, and calculates the smaller sub-triangle area as (1/2)(5)(5sqrt(3)) = 25sqrt(3)/2
distractorRationale.C: Correctly finds DB = 5 and the altitude h = 5sqrt(3), but uses the longer leg 10sqrt(3) as the height of the sub-triangle instead of the altitude, computing area as (1/2)(5)(10sqrt(3)) = 25sqrt(3)
distractorRationale.D: Correctly computes the altitude h = 5sqrt(3) but confuses the two hypotenuse segments, using the longer segment DA = 15 for the smaller triangle instead of DB = 5, computing area as (1/2)(15)(5sqrt(3)) = 75sqrt(3)/2

imageDescription: A right triangle ABC is shown with the right angle at C, hypotenuse AB = 20, angle A = 60 degrees, and angle B = 30 degrees. The shorter leg BC = 10 is opposite the 30-degree angle, and the longer leg AC = 10sqrt(3) is opposite the 60-degree angle. A dashed altitude line is drawn from C perpendicular to AB, meeting AB at point D. The segment DB = 5 is labeled near vertex B, and DA = 15 is labeled near vertex A. The altitude CD = h is labeled.
